input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property that the MSTest SDK sets automatically when it detects a test framework package reference. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- shared props for all projects -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**How it works:** The MSTest NuGet SDK sets `IsTestProject=true` for any project that references `MSTest` (or `MSTest.TestFramework`). Library/production projects won't match the condition.

**Alternative** — if you use a naming convention (e.g. `*.Tests.csproj`), you can condition on the project name instead:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
```

The `IsTestProject` approach is preferred because it's convention-independent and works with any test framework (MSTest, xUnit, NUnit).
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conventions. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of a test-related package reference or a naming convention.

The cleanest approach for MTP migration is to condition on `IsTestProject`, which MSTest SDK sets automatically, or use a naming convention.

Let me give a concise, practical answer.
